rationale: package.json declares react@99.0.0, a version that doesn't exist on the npm registry. npm install fails at the resolution step with ENOTFOUND or ETARGET, before any source code is touched. The validator's build_install layer reports the failure and short-circuits — later layers (build_compile, tests, design_fidelity) do not run because the install never completed.

baseline_comparison.eslint: Not detected — eslint operates on source code, not package.json registry resolution.
baseline_comparison.raw_llm_critique: May flag the unusual version as suspicious, but does not actually attempt to install — cannot verify the package is missing from the registry.
baseline_comparison.gpt4_critique: Same as raw_llm_critique — code review without execution.
baseline_comparison.static_tools: npm audit / npm outdated would not catch this (those work on installed packages). Only npm install itself does.
